statement.focus_question: Let $A$ be a set of $n$ integers. How many distinct $d$ can occur as the common difference of a three-term arithmetic progression in $A$?
statement.normalized: Let $A$ be a set of $n$ integers. How many distinct $d$ can occur as the common difference of a three-term arithmetic progression in $A$? In particular, are there always $O(n^{3/2})$ many such $d$?
statement.original:
Let $A$ be a set of $n$ integers. How many distinct $d$ can occur as the common difference of a three-term arithmetic progression in $A$?

In particular, are there always $O(n^{3/2})$ many such $d$?
